title: app crashes when I save
body: The app keeps crashing whenever I try to save. This is really frustrating because I keep losing my work. Please fix this ASAP.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Hi, thanks for reporting this — I understand how frustrating it is to lose work. To start narrowing this down, can you help me with a few specifics:

1. **What are you saving?** (e.g., a new task, editing an existing task, project settings, etc.)
2. **What exactly happens when it crashes?** Does the app close entirely, freeze, show an error message, or something else?
3. **When did this start?** Was saving working for you before, or has this always happened?

Any one of these would help, but all three would let us move much faster.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: I'm editing a task list — I have about 200 tasks in it. When I click Save in the toolbar, the app just closes completely. I think I see a dialog flash for a split second that mentions something about 'encoding' but it disappears too fast to read the whole thing. This started after I imported a bunch of tasks from a CSV file last week. Saving was working totally fine before that import.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for those details — that's really helpful. The timing with the CSV import and the flash of an 'encoding' message are strong clues. I have a working theory:

**The CSV file likely contained characters (such as accented letters, emoji, or special symbols) in an encoding that the app didn't convert properly on import, and now those characters cause a crash when the app tries to serialize the task list on save.**

Two quick things that would let me confirm this and get it to a developer:

1. **Does the CSV contain any non-English text, special characters, accented letters, or emoji?** (Or was it generated by a specific tool — Excel, Google Sheets, a script, etc.?)
2. **If you create a brand-new task list with just a few tasks (no CSV import) and save it, does that work fine?** This would confirm the crash is isolated to the imported data.

If you can check either of those, we should have enough to get this fixed.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: Yeah, the CSV has some special characters — things like em-dashes and curly quotes in the task names. I think it was exported from a Mac app originally. And yes, if I make a small test list without importing anything, saving works perfectly fine. Actually, even my big list saved fine *before* I did the import. I also noticed that if I remove the imported tasks, saving works again. So it's definitely something about that imported data.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: [RESOLVED] {"title":"App crashes on save when task list contains special characters imported from CSV (encoding error)","problem":"The application crashes (closes entirely) when the user clicks Save in the toolbar after importing tasks from a CSV file that contains special characters such as em-dashes and curly quotes. A dialog mentioning 'encoding' flashes briefly before the app closes. The crash is isolated to task lists containing the imported data — saving works correctly for non-imported tasks, and removing the imported tasks restores normal save behavior.","root_cause_hypothesis":"The CSV file was exported from a Mac application and likely uses a non-UTF-8 encoding (e.g., MacRoman or Windows-1252). The import process ingested the raw bytes without transcoding them to the application's internal encoding (presumably UTF-8). When the save/serialization routine encounters these improperly-encoded characters (em-dashes U+2014, curly quotes U+201C/U+201D stored as single-byte MacRoman sequences), it hits an encoding error and crashes instead of handling it gracefully.","reproduction_steps":["Create or obtain a CSV file containing special characters (em-dashes, curly quotes) exported from a Mac application (likely MacRoman or Windows-1252 encoded)","Open TaskFlow and create or open a task list","Import the CSV file into the task list using the CSV import feature","Click Save in the toolbar","Observe: app crashes with a brief 'encoding' error dialog"],"environment":"Not specified beyond the CSV originating from a Mac application. Issue is encoding-dependent, not platform-dependent.","severity":"high","impact":"Any user who imports CSV data containing non-ASCII characters from external tools (Excel on Mac, other Mac apps) will experience data loss — the app crashes on save, preventing them from persisting any changes to the affected task list. The user currently has no workaround other than removing all imported tasks.","recommended_fix":"1. Investigate the CSV import path: ensure it detects or normalizes the source encoding to UTF-8 on import (e.g., using charset detection like ICU or chardet). 2. Investigate the save/serialization path: add proper error handling so encoding failures produce a user-visible error message rather than a crash. 3. Consider adding an encoding selector to the CSV import dialog, defaulting to UTF-8 with fallback options (Windows-1252, MacRoman). 4. For already-imported bad data, provide a repair/re-encode utility or migration.","proposed_test_case":"Import a CSV file saved with MacRoman encoding containing em-dashes (0x97), curly left quotes (0x93), and curly right quotes (0x94). Verify that (a) the import correctly transcodes these to their UTF-8 equivalents (U+2014, U+201C, U+201D), and (b) the task list saves successfully without crashing. Additionally, test that a corrupted/mixed-encoding task list triggers a recoverable error message rather than a crash.","information_gaps":["Exact encoding of the source CSV file (MacRoman vs. Windows-1252 vs. other)","Exact error message in the transient dialog (only 'encoding' was partially read)","Application version and platform the reporter is running on","Whether the crash produces a crash log or stack trace that could pinpoint the exact serialization call"],"confidence":{"root_cause":"high","reproduction":"high","severity_assessment":"high"}}
